channel.title: amux Blog — AI Agent Orchestration & Parallel Coding
channel.description: Insights on parallel AI coding, agent orchestration, and developer productivity with amux.

item.title: The Self-Improving Agent Loop: How amux Gets Smarter Every Run
item.description: One command scaffolds a closed orchestrator loop: parallel agents, a verify gate, and a constraints note that accumulates lessons across runs. The swarm gets smarter every tick.

item.title: AI Coding Tools Pricing Compared (2026): What Every Setup Actually Costs
item.description: Comprehensive pricing comparison of every major AI coding tool in 2026. Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Devin, Codex, and more — including what multi-agent setups actually cost per month.

item.title: Best Multi-Agent Coding Orchestrators in 2026: Claude Squad, Conductor, Codex & More
item.description: 9 orchestrators compared on parallel capacity, git isolation, self-healing, dashboards, and unattended overnight operation. The definitive guide to running agent fleets.

item.title: Running 30 Claude Code agents on one repo without them stomping each other
item.description: Git worktrees for isolation, SQLite CAS for task claiming, SSE for real-time progress. No Redis, no Kubernetes, no microservices.

item.title: Ship Features While You Sleep
item.description: How to run AI coding agents overnight with self-healing to wake up to completed features.

item.title: How One Developer Replaces a 5-Person Team
item.description: The economics and workflow of using parallel AI agents to multiply individual output.

item.title: The Self-Healing Agent Pattern
item.description: Design patterns for building AI agents that automatically recover from failures.

item.title: Why Claude Code Sessions Crash (and How to Fix It)
item.description: Common Claude Code failure modes and how amux's self-healing handles each one automatically.

item.title: From 1 Agent to 30: Scaling Parallel Development
item.description: Lessons learned from scaling AI agent fleets from a single session to dozens.

item.title: The Case for Agent-to-Agent Communication
item.description: Why AI coding agents need to communicate with each other and how amux enables it.

item.title: Mobile-First AI Agent Management
item.description: Why managing AI agents from your phone matters and how amux's PWA enables it.

item.title: Token Economics of Parallel AI Coding
item.description: Understanding the cost structure of running multiple AI coding agents in parallel.

item.title: The End of Single-Threaded Development
item.description: Why sequential development is a bottleneck and how parallel AI agents change the equation.

item.title: Context Compaction: The Silent Killer
item.description: Why context compaction is the #1 reason AI coding agents fail and how to prevent it.

item.title: Running AI Agents 24/7
item.description: Architecture and best practices for running Claude Code agents continuously.

item.title: A Developer's Guide to Unattended Coding
item.description: Practical guide to running AI coding agents without constant supervision.

item.title: The Kanban Board Your AI Agents Actually Use
item.description: How amux's shared board enables real task coordination between AI coding agents.

item.title: Why Open Source Agent Orchestration Matters
item.description: The case for open-source, self-hosted AI agent orchestration vs. proprietary platforms.

item.title: Building Your First Agent Fleet
item.description: Step-by-step guide to setting up your first fleet of parallel AI coding agents with amux.

item.title: Vibe Coding at Scale: How AI Agent Fleets Changed My Workflow
item.description: What "vibe coding" actually means when you have 20 AI agents running in parallel.

item.title: Building with Claude 4: The Parallel Agent Workflow
item.description: How to structure your development workflow to take full advantage of Claude 4 capabilities in parallel.

item.title: MCP Servers + Parallel Agents: A Force Multiplier
item.description: How to configure MCP servers in amux sessions to give each agent specialized tool access.

item.title: The True Cost of AI Coding in 2025
item.description: A realistic breakdown of what it costs to run AI coding agents — tokens, time, and how to optimize.

item.title: The Solo Developer's AI Stack in 2025
item.description: The tools, workflows, and habits that let a solo developer ship at team speed.

item.title: How I Completed a 2-Week Sprint in 48 Hours
item.description: A real walkthrough of using an agent fleet to compress a sprint: parallel features, automated tests, and overnight runs.

item.title: Debugging When You Have 20 AI Agents
item.description: Strategies for monitoring, diagnosing, and fixing issues in a large agent fleet.

item.title: Building a SaaS MVP Overnight with Agent Fleets
item.description: How to structure an agent fleet to go from spec to deployed MVP in under 24 hours.

item.title: Context Window Strategy for Long-Running Agents
item.description: How to design prompts and tasks to keep agents productive across context boundaries.

item.title: The 2025 AI Coding Landscape: A Developer's Field Guide
item.description: A practical overview of the tools, tradeoffs, and workflows shaping AI-assisted development in 2025.
